headline: Sarkozy and the mystery of the Libyan money: A look back at a legal marathon
description: The appeals trial over suspicions of Libyan funding for the 2007 presidential campaign began on March 16. The case is filled with a cast of shady figures.

headline: Iran refuses to open Strait of Hormuz as long as US blockade remains in place
UK, France agree three-year deal to stop migrant crossings
Zelensky to join EU leaders in Cyprus after €90bn loan unblocked
description: The Ukrainian president will hold talks with EU leaders in Cyprus after Hungary's Viktor Orban lifted his veto power on a €90 billion loan for wartorn Ukraine. Orban will not attend the talks.

headline: Boris Pistorius, the man preparing Germany for war
description: The country's most popular politician for the past three years, who serves as defense minister under Chancellor Olaf Scholz, is set to unveil a military strategy review on Wednesday aimed at making the Bundeswehr the leading conventional army in Europe. Despite receiving unprecedented financial resources, the institution has been slow to reform.

headline: The playful world of British painter Rose Wylie exhibited for the first time in France
description: The 91-year-old British painter, who has become recognized late in her career and is known for her deceptively naïve style, is holding her first solo show in France at the Zwirner Gallery in Paris. There, she pays tribute to the French artist Le Douanier Rousseau, reinterpreting his work 'Mauvaise surprise' ('Unpleasant Surprise').

headline: 'I was so exhausted, I started hitting myself. They laughed': Four Cuban asylum seekers recount their ordeal at Europe's borders
description: The European Court of Human Rights is expected to rule in the coming weeks on the pushbacks experienced by four Cubans at the border between Lithuania and Belarus, against the backdrop of a hybrid war with Russia.

Neither war nor peace: Trump unilaterally extends ceasefire with Iran
description: The US president has not set a new deadline for Iran, instead waiting for the various political and security factions within the Iranian regime to reach a 'unified proposal.'

headline: Israeli films caught between calls for boycott and government threats
description: Their access to international festivals and co-productions has become increasingly complicated as Israel's image has deteriorated since the war of destruction in Gaza that followed the October 7 terrorist attack. The culture minister, for his part, is threatening to cut off state funding.

headline: France's housing crisis is straining relations between landlords and tenants
description: Tensions have risen at every stage of the landlord-tenant relationship amid a nationwide housing shortage.
